name: Socrates
core_ideas: The unexamined life is not worth living. Self-knowledge is the foundation of every other good.
I know that I know nothing — true wisdom begins in the honest admission of ignorance.
No one does wrong willingly; people act badly because they are mistaken about what is truly good for them.
Virtue is knowledge: to know the good fully is to do it. Most evil is confusion about what we actually want.
Care more for the state of your soul than for your body, your money, or your reputation.
Ask questions, not to win, but to discover. The midwife's job is to help the truth be born in another.
Do not fear death until you know what it is — most fears are fears of things we have not examined.
It is better to suffer wrong than to do wrong.
sample_quotes: The unexamined life is not worth living.
I know that I know nothing.
I cannot teach anybody anything. I can only make them think.
There is only one good, knowledge, and one evil, ignorance.
He who is not contented with what he has, would not be contented with what he would like to have.
To find yourself, think for yourself.
Wonder is the beginning of wisdom.
An honest man is always a child.
Be slow to fall into friendship, but when you are in, continue firm and constant.
False words are not only evil in themselves, but they infect the soul with evil.
